chapter: Chapter 2: The Invisible Living World: Beyond Our Naked Eye

questions.question: Explain how the invention of the microscope changed biological research.
questions.answer: The microscope allowed scientists to observe cells and microorganisms, leading to discoveries about the structure of living organisms.

questions.question: Why was Robert Hooke's discovery of cells important?
questions.answer: It revealed that living organisms are made up of basic structural units called cells.

questions.question: How did Antonie van Leeuwenhoek contribute to microbiology?
questions.answer: He built powerful microscopes and was the first to observe living microorganisms.

questions.question: Why is the nucleus called the control center of the cell?
questions.answer: Because it contains genetic material and regulates cell activities.

questions.question: Why is the cell membrane described as selectively permeable?
questions.answer: Because it allows some substances to enter or leave while blocking others.

questions.question: Explain how specialized cells help multicellular organisms function efficiently.
questions.answer: Different cell types perform specific tasks such as movement, transport, and communication.

questions.question: Why do plant cells contain chloroplasts?
questions.answer: Chloroplasts enable plants to perform photosynthesis.

questions.question: Why do plant cells have a rigid cell wall?
questions.answer: It provides structural support and protection.

questions.question: Explain the importance of decomposition in ecosystems.
questions.answer: Decomposition recycles nutrients from dead organisms back into the soil.

questions.question: Why is composting beneficial for soil fertility?
questions.answer: It enriches soil with nutrients produced by microbial decomposition.

questions.question: Why does dough rise when yeast is added?
questions.answer: Yeast fermentation releases carbon dioxide gas which expands the dough.

questions.question: How does Lactobacillus improve the nutritional value of milk?
questions.answer: It converts lactose into lactic acid and forms curd.

questions.question: Explain how nitrogen-fixing bacteria benefit plants.
questions.answer: They convert atmospheric nitrogen into compounds that plants can absorb.

questions.question: Why are microorganisms essential for nutrient cycling?
questions.answer: They break down organic matter and return nutrients to the ecosystem.

questions.question: Explain why microorganisms are considered both beneficial and harmful.
questions.answer: Some help in food production and decomposition while others cause diseases.

questions.question: Why are unicellular organisms able to survive with only one cell?
questions.answer: Their single cell performs all life processes.

questions.question: How do microorganisms contribute to the food chain?
questions.answer: They serve as food for many small organisms and recycle nutrients.

questions.question: Why are microalgae important for oxygen production?
questions.answer: They perform photosynthesis and release oxygen into water and atmosphere.

questions.question: How does microbial activity influence soil health?
questions.answer: It improves soil structure and increases nutrient availability.

questions.question: Why are microorganisms essential for maintaining ecological balance?
questions.answer: They recycle nutrients and support various biological processes.

questions.question: Explain why microorganisms are called decomposers.
questions.answer: They break down dead organic matter into simpler substances.

questions.question: How does fermentation benefit food production?
questions.answer: It enhances flavor, texture, and preservation of foods.

questions.question: Why is studying microorganisms important for medicine?
questions.answer: Understanding microbes helps scientists develop vaccines and medicines.

questions.question: How do microorganisms help maintain soil fertility?
questions.answer: They decompose organic matter and fix nitrogen.

questions.question: Why is the invisible living world important for life on Earth?
questions.answer: Microorganisms maintain ecological balance by recycling nutrients and supporting ecosystems.
